title: Experienced Cold Caller
description:
Remote | Uncapped Commission | Long-Term Opportunity



We’re a fast-growing, family-run B2B sales agency working with SaaS companies, agencies, and service businesses across the UK, Australia, Europe, and the US.



We’re looking for an experienced B2B cold caller who knows how to hold confident conversations with decision-makers, book qualified sales meetings, and take ownership of their results.



This is not an entry-level role. If you have real B2B cold calling experience and want uncapped earning potential in a supportive, high-performance environment, we want to hear from you.



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Making high-volume outbound B2B cold calls to founders, directors, and senior decision-makers

- Booking qualified sales meetings for our clients

- Following clear call frameworks while adapting naturally in conversation

- Handling objections calmly and confidently

- Keeping notes and outcomes updated in our systems

- Taking responsibility for your own performance and improvement



What We’re Looking For:

- Proven B2B cold calling experience

- Strong objection handling and conversation control

- Excellent spoken English with a clear, professional, natural tone

- Self-motivated, disciplined, and hungry to earn

- Comfortable working remotely and managing your own time

- Coachable mindset and open to feedback

- Reliable setup. Stable internet, quiet calling environment, headset



What You’ll Get:

- Uncapped commission structure

- Consistent dial volume and qualified lead lists

- Clear scripts, training, and ongoing coaching

- Long-term role with room to grow as the company scales

- Supportive, family-run culture where performance is recognised

- Work with serious B2B clients and real decision-makers



How to Apply:

Do not send a written cover letter only.



Instead, please submit:

- Your CV

- A short voice recording (2–4 minutes) covering:

- Who you are

- Why you would be great for this role

- Your experience in B2B cold calling

- The types of decision-makers you’ve spoken to before



Applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed.



If you’re confident on the phone, hungry to earn, and want to be part of a growing business where results matter, we’d love to hear you.

title: Video Editor — Stable Work, Fair Pay, and Path to Full-time (US Health Brand)
description:
Are you looking for consistent work, fair pay, and a path to full-time based on your performance? If yes, keep reading.



Most video editor jobs pay you a flat rate and that's it. Doesn't matter if your ad gets a million views or drives $50,000 in sales you get the same paycheck. No bonus. No raise. No recognition. Just the same rate, forever.



This role is different. When your ads win, you get paid more. When you prove yourself, your rate goes up. Perform well and you move to full-time. Keep growing and you become a senior editor on the team.

All AI tools are provided. You pay for nothing.

 or Instagram

title: Commercial Solar Admin (Australia)
description:
We are seeking an Australian Commercial Solar Admin Specialist with hands-on experience in Australian commercial solar projects (30 kW and above).



This role focuses on post-sale administration, approvals, compliance, and documentation for commercial solar and solar + battery projects. Once onboarded, you will be trusted to manage jobs independently within defined processes.



This role is not suitable for general VAs or candidates without direct Australian solar experience.



Key Responsibilities:



1. Commercial Solar Administration

- Manage DNSP grid connection applications for commercial solar systems (30 kW+)

- Prepare and submit pre-approval applications

- Respond to DNSP technical queries and requests for additional information

- Manage export limits, zero-export requirements, and protection conditions

- Upload and manage documentation across DNSP portals



2. Solar + Battery Projects

- Support commercial battery approvals and documentation

- Ensure batteries are included correctly in DNSP approvals and SLDs

- Manage commissioning evidence and compliance documentation

- Coordinate with installers to resolve approval or documentation gaps



3. Compliance & Documentation

Review and manage:

- Single Line Diagrams (SLDs)

- CEC-compliant documentation

- Installer and designer accreditation evidence

- Commissioning photos and reports

Prepare STC documentation for commercial systems

Package compliance folders for audit readiness



Coordination & Communication

Liaise with:

- Australian installers and electricians

- STC traders

- Retailers and DNSPs (via
 and portals)

Track job status, approvals, and outstanding actions

Flag risks, delays, or compliance issues early and escalate appropriately



Required Experience (Non-Negotiable)



Candidates must have:

- Direct experience working with Australian solar companies

- Experience supporting commercial solar systems (30 kW+)

- Hands-on experience with at least one of the following:

- Grid connection applications

- STCs for commercial solar

- Australian DNSP portals



A solid understanding of:

- Export limits and DNSP pre-approval requirements

- Solar + battery integration fundamentals

- Clean Energy Council (CEC) compliance frameworks



Essential:

Victorian solar experience, including exposure to Victorian DNSPs such as:

- Powercor

- AusNet

- Jemena

- United Energy

- Experience across multiple Australian states (VIC, NSW, QLD)

- Experience managing commercial metering coordination

- Familiarity with CRMs such as Pylon, OpenSolar, ServiceM8, or similar

- Experience supporting mid-to-high volume solar installers



*Candidates without Australian solar experience should not apply.*



Benefits

Why You’ll Love Working With Us

- AU time zone working hours (no graveyard shifts)

- We’re a passionate, forward-thinking team (and genuinely good humans)

- Growth opportunities as we expand

- Supportive culture where initiatives and ideas are celebrated



*Only applications submitted through the link will be considered. 

*Please do not apply if you don't have the qualifications, thank you!

title: Customer Support Manager
description:
Customer Support Team Manager (Ecommerce | Dropshipping | Remote)



We are hiring a Customer Support Team Manager to lead and scale support operations across multiple high-volume ecommerce brands.



This is not a ticket-queue supervisor role. We are looking for an operational leader who understands the economics of dropshipping, protects profit through dispute management, and builds systems that reduce ticket volume while improving customer satisfaction.



If you are metrics-driven, process-oriented, and experienced in multi-brand ecommerce environments, this role is for you.



What You’ll Own

1. Team Leadership & Accountability

-Lead, coach, and develop a remote CS team with clear KPIs

-Drive performance culture through structured QA and feedback

-Implement performance improvement plans when necessary

-Ensure coverage, productivity, and response time targets are consistently met



2. Systems & Operational Excellence



-Fully own and optimize Gorgias (macros, automations, tagging, workflows)

-Build and refine SOPs that reduce inefficiencies and errors

-Improve first-response time, resolution time, CSAT, and refund rates

-Continuously identify automation opportunities to reduce ticket load



3. Performance & Risk Management



-Monitor KPIs daily (AHT, FRT, resolution rate, refund rate, dispute ratio)

-Proactively flag operational risks before they impact revenue

-Deliver structured weekly reports with actionable insights



4. Chargebacks & Disputes



-Oversee disputes across payment platforms

-Maintain a 55%+ win rate

-Strengthen documentation and evidence workflows to protect revenue



What We’re Looking For



-Proven experience managing CS teams across multiple ecommerce brands

-Strong dropshipping background (private label experience is a plus)

-Advanced Gorgias expertise (not basic user level)

-Deep understanding of chargebacks and dispute handling

-Data-driven, objective leadership style

-Strong operational discipline and ownership mindset



Role Details

-Fully remote

-Full-time

-Flexible schedule, but must overlap with core business hours

-Compensation: $6–$10/hour, depending on experience and proven performance



You will have full operational ownership and the opportunity to build a high-performance support structure across multiple brands.



How to Apply



Complete the application form below:

title: Credit Repair Disputer
description:
MUST SEND A 60 SECOND LOOM WITH YOUR EXPERIENCE TO BE CONSIDERED FOR POSITION!



Virtual Assistant for Credit Repair and Financial Literacy



Location: Remote



Are you passionate about helping others achieve financial freedom and literacy? We are seeking a skilled Virtual Assistant to join our team and assist clients in disputing inaccurate credit reports and navigating the complex world of credit repair and financial literacy.



YOU MUST RECORD A 2 MIN OR 60 SECOND VIDEO EXPLAINING YOUR KNOWLEDGE AND EXPERIENCE ALONG WITH PROVEN RESULTS OF YOUR WORK OR YOU WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED.



Responsibilities:

- Dispute inaccurate credit reports on behalf of clients, adhering to the laws outlined in the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA).

- File complaints with consumer protection agencies such as the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), Better Business Bureau (BBB), and Attorney General's office when necessary.

- Utilize extensive knowledge of the credit repair process in America to provide expert guidance and support to clients.

- Communicate effectively with clients to address their concerns, answer inquiries, and provide updates on the dispute process.

- Demonstrate true determination and heart in advocating for clients' rights and financial well-being.



Qualifications:

- Previous experience working with a credit repair company, with a strong understanding of the credit repair process.

- Extensive knowledge of the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) and other relevant laws governing credit reporting and repair.

- Proven ability to file complaints with consumer protection agencies, such as the CFPB, BBB, and Attorney General's office.

- Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to empathize and connect with clients.

- True determination and heart to help clients overcome financial challenges and achieve their goals.



Benefits:

- Flexible remote work environment.

- Opportunity to make a meaningful impact in the lives of others.

- Competitive compensation package.



If you have a passion for helping others and possess the skills and qualifications listed above, we want to hear from you! Apply now to join our team and make a difference in the world of credit repair and financial literacy.



3 Commas Solutions is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity in the workplace. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, or other protected status.

description:
About Us

We are a growing UK-based group of companies operating across services and technology. We require a highly organised, detail-oriented Finance & Data Analyst to take ownership of financial administration, bookkeeping, payroll preparation, associate payments, reporting, and data accuracy across a group of companies.

Financial Administration & Bookkeeping



Full responsibility for daily and weekly bookkeeping

Loading, categorising, validating, and reconciling:



Bills

Receipts

Invoices





Obtaining missing receipts and invoices

Correct UK account assignments for tax and reporting

Weekly bank reconciliations and syncing bank accounts

Maintaining clean, audit-ready accounts at all times

Working primarily in Xero and/or QuickBooks





Associate Payments



Preparing associate payment statements

Validating calculations against source data and agreements

Reconciling associate payments to ERP data, revenue, and bank payments

Identifying and resolving discrepancies before payment

Maintaining clear, auditable records





Payroll (UK)

Required:



Preparing payroll data ready for submission to external accountants

Maintaining accurate payroll inputs:



Salaries

Contractors

Allowances and deductions





Reconciling payroll data to accounts and bank payments



Desirable:



Running UK payroll in-house

Submitting payroll using a UK payroll solution

Producing and distributing payslips to staff

Managing payroll schedules and payroll-related queries





VAT, Compliance & Statutory



Preparing and validating UK VAT returns

Ensuring correct VAT treatment across transactions

Filing annual confirmation statements

Preparing annual accounts ready for submission

Supporting compliance with UK accounting standards





Payments & Cashflow



Preparing and batching bill payments

Managing bi-weekly payment runs

Proactively reminding management of upcoming payments

Monitoring operating costs and identifying trends or risks





Financial Reporting & Data Analysis



Producing management accounts

Building financial dashboards and reports

Tracking operating costs and performance trends

Building reports using Power BI and spreadsheets

Analysing ERP and operational data for accuracy and consistency

Supporting a multi-entity group with accurate, timely data





Essential Experience

? Strong experience with Xero and/or QuickBooks accounting software

? Proven experience in UK bookkeeping

? Experience preparing payroll data for accountants

? Solid understanding of UK VAT

? Experience producing management accounts and dashboards

Desirable Skills

Who This Role Is NOT For

title: Video Editor
description:
FREELANCE VIDEO EDITOR

AI Automation & Agentic AI Content

Remote | Per-Video Contract | Daily Output



About the Role

We're building a content brand in the AI automation, agentic AI, and bespoke AI systems space — and we're looking for a freelance video editor who can keep up with daily publishing across multiple platforms. You'll receive raw footage (a mix of talking-head recordings and software screencasts) and turn it into polished, retention-optimised content ready for YouTube, Instagram Reels, TikTok, LinkedIn, and
.

This is a ground-floor opportunity. We're just launching our content operation, so you'll help shape the look and feel of the brand from day one. We're not after Hollywood production — we want an editor who understands the YouTube education and tech tutorial space, prioritises speed and viewer retention, and can deliver consistent quality at volume. Think clean, punchy, and engaging — not overproduced.

You won't be planning or filming content. We handle that. Your job is to take what we record and make it as watchable, engaging, and professional as possible — fast.



What You'll Be Editing

Long-Form YouTube Content (15–60 min)

These are the flagship videos. A mix of talking-head delivery and screen-recorded walkthroughs of AI tools, automation platforms, and custom system builds. Expect to edit:



Step-by-step tutorials showing how to build AI automations, agents, and workflows inside platforms like Make.com, n8n, or custom code environments

Strategy and explainer videos where the creator talks to camera about AI concepts, business applications, and industry developments

Client case studies and system breakdowns showing real-world AI implementations for businesses

Hybrid videos that switch between talking head and screencast throughout



What good editing looks like here:



Tight jump cuts that remove filler words, pauses, and tangents without making the video feel rushed or choppy

Strategic zoom-ins during screencasts to direct viewer attention to the specific part of the screen that matters — a node in a workflow, a line of code, a settings panel

On-screen text callouts, labels, and annotations that reinforce key points, especially when technical concepts are being explained

Strong intros (first 30 seconds) with faster pacing, pattern interrupts, or a hook that immediately signals value to the viewer

Background music (lo-fi, ambient, or electronic) mixed at appropriate levels under narration, with energy shifts matching content beats

Smooth transitions between talking-head and screencast segments so the video feels like one cohesive piece

Chapter markers and timestamps for longer tutorials



Short-Form Vertical Content (30–90 sec)

Clips repurposed from long-form recordings plus occasional standalone short recordings, distributed across Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, LinkedIn, and
. This is where most of the daily volume lives.



Identify the most compelling, self-contained moments from longer recordings — strong opinions, surprising insights, punchy tactical advice

Reframe horizontal footage to vertical 9:16 with proper framing of the speaker and any relevant screen content

Add animated/dynamic captions (bold, large, keyword-highlighted) in the style that performs well on Reels and TikTok

Craft hooks in the first 1–2 seconds: text overlays, re-ordered audio, or a punchy opening line pulled from later in the clip

Platform-native pacing — no dead frames, fast cuts, energy from the first second to the last

Adapt aspect ratios and formatting for LinkedIn and
 where vertical or square may perform differently



Thumbnails

One thumbnail per long-form video. These should be bold, high-contrast, and designed for clickthrough: large readable text, expressive creator imagery, and a consistent visual identity we'll develop together as the brand takes shape.



Required Skills



Proficiency in a professional NLE (Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve)

Proven experience editing screencast and tutorial content — you know how to make someone clicking through software actually feel engaging through zooms, callouts, pacing, and visual storytelling

Strong short-form editing skills with a portfolio of Reels/TikTok/Shorts that demonstrate retention-focused techniques (dynamic captions, hooks, fast pacing)

Thumbnail design ability using Canva, Photoshop, or Figma

Audio editing fundamentals: noise reduction, normalisation, music mixing, and handling recordings from varying environments

Ability to independently source royalty-free background music that fits a tech/education brand

Understanding of YouTube retention mechanics — you know why the first 30 seconds matter, how to structure pattern interrupts, and what makes viewers stay

Reliable and communicative — daily output means we need someone who delivers on time, every time, and flags issues early





Nice to Have



Familiarity with AI and automation tools (Make.com, n8n, Zapier, LangChain, custom AI agents) — if you understand what's on screen, you'll make smarter editing decisions about where to cut and what to emphasise

Experience with After Effects or Motion for lightweight motion graphics (animated lower-thirds, intro sequences, transitions)

Experience editing for LinkedIn — it's a different audience and tone compared to TikTok/Reels, and understanding that nuance is valuable

Comfort scaling up — we're starting here, but this role will grow as the brand grows, and we want someone who can grow with us

Familiarity with YouTube Analytics and audience retention graphs — being able to look at performance data and adjust your editing approach is a major plus





Expected Volume (to Start)



Long-form YouTube: 3–5 edited videos per week (first drafts within 24–48 hours of receiving footage)

Short-form clips: 5–7 per week across Reels, TikTok, Shorts, LinkedIn, and

Thumbnails: 1 per long-form video

Revisions: 1–2 rounds on long-form; shorts and thumbnails should be near-final on first delivery





How We'll Work Together

The workflow is straightforward. We record, you edit.



We upload raw footage (talking head, screencast, or both) to a shared drive with a brief note on the video's topic and any specific requests

You edit, add music, callouts, captions, and any necessary graphics

You deliver the edited video plus thumbnail to the shared drive

We review, provide feedback if needed, and you deliver the final version

For short-form: you identify the best clips from long-form footage and deliver batches of edited vertical content on a regular cadence



Communication will be via Slack,
, or WhatsApp — whatever works. We value editors who are proactive, flag potential issues early, and occasionally suggest improvements rather than waiting to be told.



How to Apply



Portfolio or reel showing tech/tutorial editing work — we want to see both long-form and short-form examples. Screencast editing samples are essential.

A sample edit: Find any AI/tech tutorial on YouTube, pick a 3–5 minute segment, and re-edit it the way you'd edit for us. Show us your pacing, your callouts, your zooms, and your style. Then cut a 60-second vertical short from the same footage.

Your rate (per long-form video and per short-form clip) and your realistic weekly capacity.

Your timezone and typical turnaround time for a 30–45 minute tutorial edit.





We're building something from scratch and want an editor who's excited to grow with us. If you can make a 45-minute screencast feel like 10 minutes and turn a single recording into a week's worth of shorts, we want to hear from you.

title: Accounting & Compliance Officer
description:
Job Summary:

The Accounting & Compliance Officer is responsible for overseeing bookkeeping, biweekly payroll processing, tax compliance, government remittances, financial reporting, and business permit renewals for all branches of Saiko Restaurant and Laurivia Jewelry and Accessories Shop.

This role ensures accurate financial records, timely statutory filings, and full compliance with Philippine government regulations across all business units.

________________________________________

Scope of Work

This scope of work covers ALL branches of Saiko Restaurant and Laurivia Jewelry and Accessories Shop.

________________________________________

Key Responsibilities:

1. Bookkeeping & Financial Records

• Prepare and maintain customized Excel Books of Accounts for all branches, including:

o Cash Receipts Book

o Cash Disbursement Book

o Purchase Book

o Sales Book

o Journal Book

o Payroll Summary with payslips

• Generate consolidated and per-branch monthly and quarterly financial reports:

o Profit & Loss Statement

o Balance Sheet

o Statement of Cash Flows

• Prepare templates for vouchers, liquidation reports, and ageing of receivables/payables, when necessary.

• Implement and monitor voucher system for all cash disbursements to ensure proper control and documentation.

________________________________________

2. Payroll Processing & Government Remittances

• Process payroll on a biweekly basis for all branches.

• Collect and review Daily Time Records (DTR) three (3) days before payroll release.

• Prepare a draft payroll summary one (1) to two (2) days before the scheduled payroll date.

• Ensure that the drafted payroll summary is reviewed and approved by the HR Manager and Branch Owners prior to payroll release.

• Prepare individual payslips and payroll summary reports.

• Compute, withhold, and remit mandatory government contributions, including:

o Social Security System (SSS)

o Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (PhilHealth / PHIC)

o Home Development Mutual Fund (HDMF / Pag-IBIG)

• Address employee concerns related to government benefits, including SSS salary loan applications and other statutory inquiries.

________________________________________

3. BIR Compliance & Tax Filing

• Prepare, file, pay, and properly store all monthly, quarterly, and annual BIR returns for all branches, including but not limited to:

o Quarterly Income Tax Return (1701Q)

o VAT Return (2550Q)

o Annual Income Tax Return (1701)

o BIR Form 2316

• Prepare and submit required attachments such as:

o VAT RELIEF

o Alphalist of Suppliers

o Annual Alphalist of Compensation

o Annual Inventory List

• Issue BIR Form 2307 to suppliers.

• Compile and monitor creditable withholding tax certificates received from customers.

• Coordinate with external auditors if required (excluding annual audit fees).

________________________________________

4. Management Reporting & Financial Advisory

• Provide branch-level and consolidated financial analysis.

• Participate in financial planning and budgeting for both business entities.

• Provide consultation on cost monitoring, pricing strategy, and financial performance improvement.

________________________________________

5. Business Permit & Regulatory Assistance

• Assist in the annual renewal of business permits for all branches.

• Prepare and provide required supporting documents for permit processing.

________________________________________

Qualifications:

• Bachelor’s Degree in Accountancy, Accounting Technology, or related field.

• Proven experience in multi-branch bookkeeping and payroll processing.

• Knowledgeable in Philippine taxation and statutory compliance.

• Proficient in Microsoft Excel and financial report preparation.

• Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of handling multiple entities simultaneously.

title: Experienced Graphic Designer / Photo Retoucher
description:
We’re hiring an experienced Graphic Designer and Photo Retoucher with real estate experience who understands how to create clean, professional, high-converting visuals across digital and print.



This role is for someone who knows how to design with clarity and purpose. You understand branding, layout, hierarchy, and how visuals influence attention and trust, especially in real estate marketing.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Team Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important, and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just managing marketing campaigns for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have real estate design experience and understand what makes listings visually credible and market-ready? Do you know how to design social posts and brochures that look clean, professional, and on-brand without overcomplicating them? Do you move fast when timelines are tight and still pay attention to spacing, alignment, and small details?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Social & Digital Design

- Design YouTube branding assets including thumbnails, banners, and episode graphics.

- Create Instagram listing posts, announcements, ads, and story graphics.

- Develop and maintain branded social templates for consistent output.



Print Design

- Design real estate yard signs and signage.

- Create listing brochures and marketing flyers.

- Prepare clean, print-ready files with correct sizing and bleed settings.



Real Estate Photo Editing

- Edit listing photos for lighting, color correction, and clarity.

- Ensure final images look professional while remaining realistic.



Brand Consistency & Organization

- Maintain consistent fonts, colors, and layout standards

- Organize design files clearly for future use



Requirements:

- Proven experience in real estate graphic design, required

- Strong portfolio showing real estate social and print work, required

- Experience with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Lightroom), or similar tools

- Strong understanding of layout, spacing, and visual hierarchy

- Experience preparing print-ready files

- Experience editing real estate photos

- Ability to work quickly without sacrificing quality

- Strong communication skills

- Ability to follow brand guidelines

- A stable, high-speed internet connection

- Growth mindset



BONUS SKILLS (NOT REQUIRED)

- Light motion graphics

- Understanding of social media performance metrics

- Basic copy awareness (headline spacing, readability, CTA placement)



NOTE: This is a project-based role with the opportunity to grow into a full-time position for the right person.



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.







STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $20-40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Graphic Designer with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Product Assistant Role
description:
Join one of Australia's fastest-growing travel businesses!



We're a dynamic and successful team that's been making waves in the travel industry for the past four years. We have a fantastic, high-energy work culture and a team that is focused on success—we're breaking new records every week and month!



This is a ground-floor opportunity to build an incredible career in travel. As we continue to grow, you'll be in a great position to grow with us.



???? About the Role



We are seeking a highly meticulous and organised Product Assistant to join our busy team.

In this critical role, you will be the backbone of our product offerings. You will be responsible for ensuring all our amazing travel packages are accurately loaded, maintained, and priced in our systems. Your work will directly impact what our customers see and book, making your attention to detail paramount to our success.

You will be working with our internal booking system and Google Sheets daily.



Key Responsibilities

- Accurately upload new travel packages, including descriptions, inclusions, and images, into our system.

- Carefully review and interpret supplier contracts to extract key information (e.g., rates, blackout dates, terms).

- Conduct regular checks and audits to ensure all live pricing and availability are correct and up-to-date.

- Liaise with travel service providers to assist in uploading new travel packages and maintaining existing ones.

- Manage and organise product data using Google Sheets.

- Assist the wider product and operations team with various administrative tasks as required.



About You



To be successful in this role, you must be a systems-oriented person who thrives on accuracy.



? Non-Negotiables (Must-Haves):

- Exceptional Attention to Detail: You must be meticulous, spotting errors that others might miss. This is the most important quality for this role.

- Google Sheets Proficiency: You are highly confident working with spreadsheets, including data entry, basic formulas, and data filtering.

- Excellent English Skills: You have strong written and verbal communication skills.

- An Organised Mindset: You can manage multiple tasks and prioritise effectively in a fast-paced environment.



? Bonus Points (Nice-to-Haves):



Travel Industry Experience: A background in the travel industry (especially Australian travel) is a huge plus!

Australian Business Experience: Familiarity with working for an Australian-based company.

Systems Experience: Any previous experience with booking systems or databases is highly regarded.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

mbers to unblock tasks

title: Affiliate Manager
description:
Job Description:



Full-Time | Work From Home | Ecommerce & Influencer Partnerships



SRI Labs is a fast-growing, premium beauty & wellness ecommerce brand known for high-end skincare, hair tools, and breakthrough consumer products. We’re looking for a driven, organized, and relationship-focused Affiliate Manager to lead and scale our affiliate program across platforms like ShopMy, Levanta, and key influencer networks.



If you love partnering with creators, optimizing affiliate performance, and helping a brand grow through relationships — this is the role for you.



What You’ll Do



As our Affiliate Manager, you will own the entire affiliate ecosystem and work directly with the CEO and marketing leadership team to scale creator-led revenue.



Key Responsibilities



Manage and optimize SRI Labs’ affiliate program across ShopMy, Levanta, and other affiliate platforms.

Recruit, vet, and onboard new affiliates, creators, and influencers that align with our brand.

Build strong long-term relationships with partners to drive consistent revenue and brand growth.

Track performance, payouts, ROI, and ensure all affiliates are supported with assets, codes, and campaigns.

Coordinate affiliate-driven launches, seasonal promotions, and strategic campaigns.

Identify top performers and build incentive strategies to maximize their output.

Maintain reporting dashboards and communicate results to leadership weekly.

Collaborate with internal marketing, product, design, and operations teams to support affiliate needs.

Continuously test new platforms, creators, strategies, and incentives to grow revenue.

Who You Are



We’re looking for someone who is:



Based in the Philippines

Experienced in affiliate, influencer marketing, or digital partnerships (1–3+ years preferred).

Organized, reliable, and able to manage many creators and campaigns at once.

Strong at relationship-building — someone affiliates love to work with.

Comfortable working within platforms like ShopMy, Levanta, Impact, or similar tools.

Data-driven with excellent follow-through and communication skills.

Hungry, proactive, and passionate about helping a brand grow.

Why Join SRI Labs?



Remote work

Work directly with a high-performing founding team

Competitive salary based on experience

Long-term growth potential within a fast-scaling ecommerce company

Supportive, positive culture built around speed, innovation, and passion for great products

Opportunity to make a big impact — your work will directly drive revenue and brand expansion

How to Apply



 your resume and a quick 2–3 sentence introduction 



Subject Line: Affiliate Manager – [Your Name]



Include:



Links to affiliate programs or creators you’ve worked with

Any relevant platforms/tools you’ve used

Your expected monthly salary (in PHP)

Job Type: Full-time

title: Social Media Manager
description:
We are looking for a Social Media Manager to join our team!



About the Role:

We are looking for a creative and detail oriented Social Media Manager to manage our online presence across key platforms. This role is responsible for planning, writing, and publishing engaging content while maintaining a consistent brand voice and messaging.



The role also includes copywriting for social media posts and ads, so we are looking for someone who can create content that is not only engaging but also supports campaign goals and audience growth.



Key Responsibilities:

• Manage social media platforms such as
, Instagram, and TikTok

• Plan, write, and publish engaging content for social media posts

• Write clear and compelling copy for ads, captions, hooks, and promotional content

• Maintain posting schedules and ensure brand consistency across platforms

• Support content planning for campaigns, promotions, and audience engagement

• Track content performance and engagement metrics



Requirements:

• Proven experience in managing social media platforms

• Excellent command of written English and grammar

• Strong copywriting, creative writing, and research skills

• Able to write engaging content for both social media posts and ad campaigns

• Highly organized, detail oriented, and able to meet deadlines

• Basic knowledge of content analytics and performance tracking

• Familiarity with tools such as Semrush, Later, Meta Business Suite, Wix, and Klaviyo



Working days and hours:

• Full-time basis – 40 hrs/week (Mon–Fri)

• Working hours: 10 am to 6:30 pm (30 min lunch break)

• 14 days of Paid Annual Leave and Paid Public Holiday Benefits (all major holidays in the Philippines) included



Application Instructions:

Please submit your application with the following (applications without these will be less considered):

• Your CV/Resume.

• A short video introduction of yourself and why you’re a good fit.

• Why you are leaving your last job or what you were doing just before this application.

• Your relevant past experience (if any) and why you’d be the best fit for this role.

If you do not have experience, please be honest and state so as well.

* Applications without the above 4 points will be less considered.



P.S. Please format your replies in point form according to the 4 items asked above,

e.g.: 1. My resume is here:

2. Video introduction here:

3. I am doing… etc

4. I have… etc The more information you provide in your initial application, the better.

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Advanced Davinci Resolve Studio Editor (Real Estate + Motion Graphics Editor)
description:
We are a growing real estate media company specializing in cinematic, modern real estate listing videos and personal brand talking-head content. We work with top realtors, construction teams, and professional brands, and we are building systems that allow us to scale without sacrificing quality.

This editor role is critical to our growth. You will help maintain our visual standards, execute repeatable workflows, and ensure every project meets our quality expectations while delivering a 10/10 client experience.

Quality is non-negotiable. We do not cut corners and expect the same standard from everyone on our team.

We are looking for a DaVinci Resolve Studio Video Editor who has strong fundamentals and is actively leveling up into more advanced execution, especially with motion titles and modern social media styles.

Important:

To confirm attention to detail, please include the word “Resolve” in the first line of your application message. Applications without this will not be reviewed.



Responsibilities:

- Edit real estate listing videos with cinematic pacing, clean color, and polished audio.

- Edit talking-head videos with modern motion titles and simple animations inspired by trending social media styles.

- Execute consistent edits using provided LUTs, templates, and SOPs.

- Deliver projects on fast turnaround timelines (typically 24–48 hours).

- Maintain organization, consistency, and quality across repeat projects.



Job Requirements:

- Solid experience editing in DaVinci Resolve Studio. Studio version is required. 

- Comfortable creating clean motion titles and simple animations (lower thirds, callouts, emphasis text).

- Efficient color grading workflow and clean audio handling.

- Strong communication skills and proficiency in English.

- Willing to learn and work within our project management software, ClickUp.

- Ability to work within U.S. business hours (PST) or overlap when needed.

- High attention to detail and ability to take feedback well.



Culture Fit (Non-Negotiable):

- Punctual and reliable.

- Coachable and committed to continuous improvement.

- Positive, solutions-focused attitude.

- No ego — just excellence.



Compensation & Growth:

- Per gig position. Potential to grow into a part-time and/or full-time editor after working together for some time.

- Starts with a free test edit. If hired, you will be compensated for the edit. 

- Clear systems, SOPs, and feedback provided to help you improve.

- Opportunity for long-term work, increased responsibility, and higher compensation as quality and efficiency improve.



How to Apply:

Please send the following;

- A short introduction about yourself.

- Relevant work samples (real estate, talking-head, or social content preferred).

- Confirmation that you can meet 24–48 hour turnaround times and work within PST hours when required.

This is not a small opportunity.

Local businesses routinely recover significant lost revenue while paying only $250–$500 per month.

Clients stay.

**Client Retention is 90%

Performance Bonuses 



???? Daily Expectations

 campaigns

 templates

title: Website Production & Quality Specialist
description:
About the Role



We are hiring a Website Production & Quality Specialist to review, refine, and finalize websites before they are delivered to customers. Your responsibility is to ensure every site meets our internal quality checklist and standards for layout, structure, clarity, and consistency.



This is a hands-on role inside our WYSIWYG editor. You will not simply point out issues. You will fix them.



We are looking for someone who is able to commit to this role full-time and treat it as their primary professional focus.



This is a remote contract role.



What You’ll Do



• Review completed websites against a defined quality checklist

• Work directly inside our editor to correct layout, spacing, formatting, and structural issues

• Ensure headings, sections, links, and metadata are implemented correctly

• Improve unclear or awkward copy where needed

• Ensure consistency across all pages within a site

• Perform final quality checks before sites are considered complete

• Identify recurring issues and suggest improvements to internal processes



Required Skills and Experience



• Exceptional attention to detail. You naturally notice spacing inconsistencies, formatting errors, broken links, and structural issues

• Direct experience using WYSIWYG website editors or builders

• Confidence making layout and content edits independently

• Strong written English and ability to improve clarity

• Ability to work methodically through structured checklists without missing small issues

• Availability to commit full-time to this role as your primary professional focus



Nice to Have



• Experience reviewing small business websites

• Background in website production, publishing, or editorial roles

• Basic understanding of SEO structure and page hierarchy



Working Hours and Location



• Remote role based in the Philippines

• Core working hours aligned with EST

• First two weeks of training may require availability during PST hours

Looking For:

Python (Core Requirement)

description:

Most of the work will involve finding people in

•
 groups that do review-for-review

 pages related to review exchanges

 and

title: EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT / OPERATIONS COORDINATOR
description:
ABOUT US (please read this full job description carefully)



LYNKS AI Agency is a fast-growing marketing agency specializing in lead generation for real estate and property management clients. We're scaling rapidly (5 active clients, adding more monthly) and our founder needs someone to manage his calendar, tasks, finances, and operations so he can focus on strategy and client relationships.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



Calendar Management:



• Manage calendars in Google Calendar AND GoHighLevel

• Schedule client calls, tea
etings, and personal appointments

• Send reminders before important meetings

• Coordinate time zones and avoid scheduling conflicts

Google Drive Organization:



• File organization and document management

• Keep Drive folders clean and structured

• Ensure easy access to important files

Time Management & Task Lists:



• Manage ClickUp tasks (or similar platforms like Asana, Trello, Monday)

• Keep founder on track with daily/weekly priorities

• Follow up on overdue tasks (gentle accountability)

• Track project deadlines and deliverables

Excel & Expense Tracking:



• Update Excel/Google Sheets spreadsheets

• Track expenses and categorize transactions

• Monitor recurring payments

• Organize receipts and financial data

Payment Tracking:



• Track client payments (who paid, who's overdue)

• Send invoice reminders

• Monitor subscription payments

Administrative Support:



•
 management (flag important messages)

• Follow-ups (make sure nothing falls through cracks)

• General organization and coordination



REQUIREMENTS



Must Have:



• 3+ years experience as Executive Assistant or Virtual Assistant

• Google Calendar AND GoHighLevel calendar management (must be comfortable with both)

• Google Drive organization (filing, folder structure, document management)

• ClickUp or similar task management (Asana, Trello, Monday, etc.)

• Excel/Google Sheets proficiency (formulas, tracking, data entry)

• Expense tracking and basic accounting

• Fluent English (written and spoken)

• Detail-oriented (nothing slips through the cracks)

• Proactive communication (remind before things are late, not after)

• Reliable internet and able to work Canadian business hours (8am-5pm EST)



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



You're the right fit if you:



• Are extremely organized and detail-oriented

• Have experience managing calendars in multiple platforms

• Take initiative without being asked

• Can work independently with minimal supervision

• Think ahead and anticipate needs

• Are calm under pressure and handle multiple priorities

• Care about keeping things running smoothly



COMPENSATION & BENEFITS



• Pay: $500 USD/month (paid monthly via Wise or PayPal)

• Hours: 40 hours/week, flexible within EST business hours

• Growth: As the agency grows, this role can expand with more responsibility

• Stability: Long-term position for the right person



HOW TO APPLY



Send a message with:



1. Experience Summary:  • How many years as EA/VA?

  • Have you managed calendars in GoHighLevel before?

  • What task management tools have you used?

  • Do you have Google Drive organization experience?



2. Answers to these questions:  • Describe a time you caught something that almost fell through the cracks. How did you handle it?

  • How do you prioritize when everything feels urgent?

  • What's your availability? Can you work 8am-5pm EST overlap hours?



3. References:  • At least 1 previous client/employer who can vouch for your work



IMPORTANT - READ CAREFULLY:



To show you've read this entire posting thoroughly, please include the phrase "Blue Pineapple" somewhere in your application message. Applications without this phrase will not be considered.



4. Short intro:  • Why you're interested in this role

  • What makes you a great fit



Subject line: "Executive Assistant - [Your Name]"

We'll review applications within 3-5 days and reach out to top candidates for an interview.



- Austin Furtado, Founder

LYNKS AI Agency

title: High-Accountability Fleet Maintenance Coordinator (FMC)
description:
We don’t need a "helper"; we need a leader to take full ownership of our fleet maintenance. Your job is simple but demanding: Ensure our vehicles are 100% road-ready and our maintenance team (mechanics/inspectors) hits every deadline. No excuses.



Why You?



You are bored by easy jobs.



You naturally take charge and don't mind being the "bad guy" to hit a deadline.



You hate inefficiency and disorganization.



You prefer to be judged by your results, not your hours.



Your Command:



Oversee the Entire Lifecycle: You own the maintenance process from the 
nt a vehicle hits 4,900 miles until the service is closed.



Drive Accountability: You manage the mechanics and inspectors. If they are behind, you get them back on track.



Execute the Plan: Coordinate repairs, monitor performance, and ensure every task is done to our high standard.



Solve Problems: When a vehicle goes down, you find the fastest path to getting it back to our members.



Requirements:



Assertive Communication: You can handle tough conversations with technicians.



Systems Expert: You must be proficient in fleet software (Fleetio experience is a massive plus).



Decisive: You don't wait to be told what to do; you see the gap and fill it.



The No-Go List:



If you need your hand held, do not apply.



If you struggle with giving direct feedback, do not apply.



If you are not comfortable using technology to track every bolt and oil change, do not apply.



To Be Considered:

We move fast, and we expect our leaders to do the same.



Submit a Video Intro: Record a 2-minute video of yourself (use Loom, YouTube, or Google Drive).



The Content: Tell me exactly how you will ensure our mechanics hit their deadlines and how your leadership will keep our fleet at 100% readiness for our members.

title: Brand & Visual Identity Designer (I/C)
description:
Remote | Philippines-Based | Full-Time | U.S. Company



We are a U.S.-based organization entering a new growth phase and seeking a full-time Brand & Visual Identity Designer to help shape and elevate our brand direction.



This is not a basic graphic design role.



We are looking for someone who understands how visuals influence decision-making, how design positions authority, and how brand identity impacts perception.



You will help rebrand sales materials, refine visual identity, and support ongoing brand marketing initiatives.



What This Role Is



• Brand positioning through design

• Sales deck redesign with emotional intelligence

• Strategic image selection

• Visual storytelling

• Building consistency across all brand assets

• Supporting long-term brand direction



What This Role Is NOT



• Basic Canva editing

• Logo-only design

• Order-taking without strategic thinking

• Template recycling



We need someone who understands WHY certain visuals work and how design influences trust, credibility, and action.



Responsibilities



• Redesign and elevate sales presentations

• Develop brand identity consistency across materials

• Select and position images strategically

• Create marketing assets aligned with positioning goals

• Collaborate on visual direction for new initiatives

• Maintain brand cohesion across platforms

• Provide creative input on visual strategy



Requirements



• Strong portfolio demonstrating brand identity work

• Understanding of emotional design and buyer psychology

• Experience creating high-impact sales decks

• Ability to explain design decisions logically

• Strong communication skills

• Ability to work independently

• Reliable internet connection

• Professional home office setup



Work Structure



• Full-time, long-term position

• Initial overlap required with U.S. Eastern Time

• Hybrid flexibility once working relationship is established

• Collaboration during U.S. business hours as needed



Design work may be completed during your preferred creative hours, provided deadlines and collaboration needs are met.



Compensation



• Competitive monthly salary (based on experience)

• Long-term creative partnership opportunity

• Opportunity to shape brand direction at a strategic level



Application Instructions (Mandatory – Read Carefully)



To be considered, you MUST complete ALL of the following:



Submit your resume



Submit your portfolio (must include brand identity and presentation work)



Submit a 1–2 minute video introduction



Complete the short online DISC assessment (5–10 minutes) here:







Applications missing required steps will NOT be reviewed.



Video Instructions



In your 1–2 minute video, please include:



• Your approach to brand positioning

• How design influences emotion and decision-making

• An example of a project where you intentionally used visuals to drive a specific response



We are looking for strategic thinking, not just technical skill.



This role is ideal for someone who sees design as positioning, not decoration. If you think in terms of perception, influence, and brand authority, we encourage you to apply.

This role is critical to our operations. The person in this position will be responsible for reviewing client credit reports, identifying changes, updating our CRM system, and coordinating daily dispute activity with our dispute managers.

Location

Remote



Schedule

Monday – Friday

ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES

• Deleted accounts

---



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

Experience working with CRM systems such as:



HubSpot

Dispute Fox

or similar client management systems



Strong computer and document management skills

Comfortable working in a structured workflow environment



---



PERSONAL QUALITIES WE VALUE



Highly detail oriented

Professional and organized

---



HOW TO APPLY

title: LinkedIn Ghostwriter — AI & Automation Agency
description:
The Role

We're looking for someone to fully own and manage the founder's personal LinkedIn profile. The founder runs an automation agency serving e-commerce and logistics companies — so the content needs to reflect deep knowledge of AI, automation, and how businesses actually use these tools to grow. This isn't generic "5 tips for productivity" content. We need someone who understands the AI and automation space, follows the trends daily, and can turn that knowledge into scroll-stopping LinkedIn posts.



What You'll Do



Write and publish 4–5 LinkedIn posts per week in the founder's voice — a mix of insights, hot takes, case studies, and thought leadership around AI and automation

Stay on top of AI news and trends (new tools, use cases, industry shifts) and turn them into timely, relevant content

Engage daily on target prospects' and industry leaders' posts with thoughtful, value-adding comments — not generic fluff

Manage inbound DMs and send personalized outreach to potential clients and partners

Grow the founder's network strategically — connecting with e-commerce operators, logistics companies, agency owners, and tech decision-makers

Track key metrics weekly: follower growth, post impressions, engagement rate, DM response rate

Collaborate with the founder on content ideas — bring topics to the table, don't wait to be told what to write





Requirements



1+ year managing LinkedIn profiles (ghostwriting, personal branding, or agency experience)

Genuinely knowledgeable about AI and automation — you follow this space because you're interested, not because it's a job requirement

Can write in a natural, conversational, opinionated tone — not corporate, not ChatGPT-sounding

Understands the LinkedIn algorithm — what gets reach, what gets engagement, what gets ignored

Proactive and self-managing — you plan the content calendar, not the founder

Strong written English with the ability to match someone else's voice

Familiar with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, n8n, Make, etc.) — bonus if you actually use them



Apply here:

title: Operations Manager — Automation Agency
description:
Operations Manager Automation Agency |

Hours: Mon–Fri, EST business hours overlap required





Who We Are

We're a growing automation agency that builds custom n8n workflows, API integrations, and AI-powered systems for e-commerce and logistics companies. 



Who You Are

You're the person who walks into chaos and creates order — without being told how. You've managed operations at an agency or tech company before, you understand automation and APIs at a working level, and you don't need hand-holding to figure out what needs to happen next. You see a gap, you fill it. You see a fire, you put it out. You're the adult in the room.

This isn't a VA role. This is a right-hand operator position. You'll own the day-to-day operations so the founder can focus on selling and building.



What You'll Own



Client management — Be the main point of contact for active clients. Handle updates, requests, timelines, and expectations so the founder isn't in every Slack thread and
 chain.

Project management — Track all active builds across clients. Know what's on track, what's behind, and what's blocked — before anyone has to ask.

Scoping and QA — Review client requests, scope out what needs to be built, and QA completed n8n workflows and integrations before delivery. You don't need to be the best builder, but you need to understand what you're looking at.

Team management — Manage VAs and contractors. Assign tasks, review output, hold people accountable. Eventually, hire new tea
mbers as we grow.

Building systems — Create the SOPs, processes, and documentation that don't exist yet. Build the operating system for the agency from the ground up.

Light building — Handle simpler n8n workflows and automation builds independently, freeing the founder for complex architecture work.





Requirements



3+ years in operations, project management, or agency management

Working knowledge of automation tools — n8n, Make, or Zapier (you don't need to be an expert builder, but you need to understand workflows, APIs, and webhooks)

Experience managing remote teams (VAs, contractors, or developers)

Comfortable communicating directly with US-based clients via Slack,
, and occasional video calls

Highly organized with a track record of building processes where none existed

Self-directed — you figure out what needs to happen and do it without waiting for instructions

Strong written and spoken English

title: MCA Underwriter | Full-Time EST
description:
Hours: Mon–Fri, EST business hours overlap required





About the Role

We're a merchant cash advance company looking for a skilled underwriter to join our team. You'll be responsible for reviewing MCA applications, analyzing merchant financials, assessing risk, and making funding recommendations. This is a high-volume, fast-paced role where speed and accuracy both matter. You'll work directly with our sales and funding teams to move deals through the pipeline efficiently.



What You'll Do



Review and analyze incoming MCA applications and supporting documents

Examine 3–6 months of business bank statements to evaluate cash flow, daily balances, and revenue trends

Identify red flags such as NSFs, negative balances, stacking, and undisclosed positions

Calculate key metrics including average daily balance, monthly revenue, and deposit consistency

Determine funding amounts, hold-back percentages, and terms based on risk assessment

Issue approvals, declines, or request additional documentation with clear reasoning

Communicate underwriting decisions to the sales team with turnaround expectations

Maintain accurate records and notes in the CRM/underwriting platform

Meet daily volume targets (typically 15–25+ applications per day)

Stay current on MCA industry trends, compliance requirements, and risk factors





Requirements

Must-haves:



2+ years of hands-on MCA or alternative lending underwriting experience

Strong ability to read and interpret business bank statements quickly

Understanding of MCA-specific risk factors — stacking, split funding, seasonal businesses, etc.

Experience calculating daily balances, average monthly revenue, and position exposure

High attention to detail with the ability to spot inconsistencies and fraud indicators

Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-volume environment

Reliable internet connection and quiet workspace

Available during US business hours (at least 9 AM–5 PM EST)

Strong written English for internal communication and deal notes



Nice-to-haves:



Experience with underwriting tools like DecisionLogic, Plaid, Ocrolus, or LendingFront

Familiarity with CRMs such as Salesforce, HubSpot, or industry-specific platforms

Background in other alternative lending products (revenue-based financing, business lines of credit, equipment financing)

Experience training or mentoring junior underwriters

Knowledge of UCC filings and lien searches





What We Offer



Stable, long-term full-time position — not a short-term project

Clear underwriting guidelines and support — you won't be guessing

Opportunity to grow into a senior or lead underwriter role

A team that values speed, good judgment, and reliability



How to Apply:

title: Outbound Sales Caller

title: Full-Time Video Editor (After Effects + Premiere)
description:
True crime channel. Documentary-grade production. We have standards and every video needs to reflect that. Each video is 30–45 minutes, documentary style with talking head host. You handle everything from custom After Effects intros to the full edit.



How it works:

You'll receive scripts with editing direction, an asset library per case (b-roll clips, images, talking head footage, screenshots). Most assets are provided but manual searching and sourcing will still be needed from you to fill gaps.



The intro (60 sec) gets the premium editing, custom motion graphics, text reveals, sound design, transitions in After Effects. Every intro is unique, not copy-paste templates.



The body edit is clean minimal documentary-style work in Premiere — b-roll matched to talking head narration, cutaways at the right 
nts, photo keyframes, text overlays for names/dates/locations.



Requirements:

- Strong After Effects — real motion graphics, not just Premiere with plugins

- Premiere for long-form timeline editing

- Understanding of YouTube pacing and retention

- Good PC specs

- Reliable internet for daily large file transfers

- Available to start asap



Payment:

$800/mo, full time



Note: NDA + Hubstaff required



To apply:

- Examples of custom After Effects intros (true crime or documentary style preferred, if you have it)

- 1 example of a long-form edit (10+ min)

- Your current PC specs (CPU, RAM, GPU, storage, internet speed)

- When you can start



To confirm you read this entire listing, put in your proposal subject the word "FOCUS"

title: Laravel Developer
description:
We are currently seeking a talented Laravel Developer to join our team for a full-time position. The ideal candidate should be proficient in Laravel, PHP, and VueJS, and have experience working in a fast-paced development environment.



Responsibilities:



- Develop and maintain web applications using Laravel and PHP

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and implement new features

- Participate in code reviews and provide feedback to ensure code quality and adherence to best practices

- Write clean, reusable, and efficient code

- Troubleshoot and debug issues in production environments

- Stay up-to-date with emerging trends in web development



Requirements:



- Proven experience as a Laravel Developer or similar role

- Strong knowledge of PHP, VueJS, Inertia and related web technologies

- Familiarity with MySQL and database design principles

- Experience with Git version control

- Strong problem-solving and analytical skills

- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment

- Excellent verbal and written communication skills

- BSc degree in Computer Science or a related field



Working Hours:



- This is a full-time position, working weekdays from 9am to 5:30 pm GMT+8.





If you meet the above requirements and are passionate about web development, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity. 

Please submit your resume and a cover letter explaining why you would be the ideal candidate for this role.

title: Real Estate Data Manager
description:
Company Overview: We are a fast-growing real estate investment firm focused on data-driven strategies to make informed decisions. We leverage technology to streamline processes, maximize lead generation, and drive better client and partner outcomes. We're looking for a skilled Real Estate Data Analyst who can help us make the most out of our platforms and enhance the efficiency of our operations.



Position Overview: We seek an experienced and detail-oriented Real Estate Data Analyst who is highly proficient in using tools such as Podio, Xencall, DealMachine, and REI Sift to gather, analyze, and manage real estate data. This role is ideal for someone who has hands-on experience in real estate data analysis and is comfortable working with CRM and lead generation tools in a fast-paced environment.



Key Responsibilities:



*Analyze, clean, and manage real estate data to support decision-making and strategy development.

*Manage and optimize our CRM system (Podio), ensuring efficient workflows and tracking of leads, deals, and tasks.

*Utilize Xencall to monitor and enhance our communication strategies for contacting and following up with leads.

*Integrate DealMachine to ensure optimal lead generation, tracking, and driving ROI on direct mail campaigns.

*Work with REI Sift to filter, clean, and sort lead lists, ensuring high-quality lead acquisition for our real estate business.

*Provide data insights and recommendations to the team based on trends and patterns observed in the data.

*Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to track KPIs, marketing campaigns, and real estate investment performance.

*Collaborate with other departments (sales, marketing, acquisitions) to improve processes and outcomes.



Required Skills & Qualifications:



*Proven experience as a Real Estate Data Analyst or in a similar role within the real estate investment industry.

*In-depth experience with Podio, Xencall, DealMachine, and REI Sift tools.

*Strong analytical skills with the ability to turn data into actionable insights.

*Expertise in Excel, Google Sheets, or similar data management tools for data cleaning and analysis.

*Excellent organizational and time-management skills with an ability to manage multiple priorities.

*Familiarity with real estate investment strategies, including direct mail marketing, lead generation, and CRM workflows.

*Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively with remote teams.

*Detail-oriented and self-motivated, with a proactive approach to problem-solving.

PowerPoint)

title: Video Editor & Image Designer
description:
ABOUT US 

We are a fast-growing DTC skincare eCommerce brand. We are looking for a long-term tea
mber, not a freelancer, who wants to build a stable career with us.



We believe in empowering our team. You will get access (and training!) on premium AI tools like Higgsfield, Gemini, and ChatGPT to make your workflow faster and more creative.



WHO YOU ARE 

We are looking for a "preditor" (Producer/Editor)—someone who understands storytelling, pacing, and marketing, not just button-pushing.



- You possess a "student mindset": You take feedback well, you don't take critiques personally, and you improve quickly. We are looking for someone who wants to learn and grow, not someone who thinks they already know everything.

- You are efficient: You can edit a high-quality 60-second video in 2-3 hours and create a social graphic in 1-2 hours without sacrificing quality.

- You are detail-oriented: You have 0% tolerance for typos in captions and overlays.

- You are tech-savvy: You are excited to use AI to generate ideas and speed up your work.



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

- Short-Form Video: Edit engaging Reels, Shorts, and TikToks (15–90 seconds) focused on UGC, products, and trends.

- Long-Form Video: Edit YouTube videos, and VSLs (5+ minutes) with clean pacing and storytelling.

- Engagement: Add animated captions, text callouts, B-roll, and visual hooks to keep retention high.

- Graphic Design: Create static ad creatives, quote cards, thumbnails, and simple motion ads.

- Photo Editing: Retouch, resize, and clean up product/lifestyle images.

- Copywriting: Write and polish short English hooks/headlines for your videos and ads.



TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS (Must Have)

- Computer Specs: You must have a PC capable of editing 4K footage and running Adobe Premiere Pro/After Effects without lag or crashing. (Recommended: 16GB+ RAM, SSD Storage).

- Internet Connection: You must have a reliable, high-speed internet connection (Fiber preferred). You will be required to download and upload large batches of raw 4K video files from Google Drive daily.

- Software: Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro (preferred), CapCut, or Final Cut + Canva/Photoshop/Figma or equivalent.



WHY JOIN US? 

- Day Shift Schedule: Enjoy your evenings and weekends! Work normal PH business hours. 

- AI Training: We pay for the tools and teach you how to use them. 

- Stability: We are looking for a partner for the long haul. 

- Portfolio: Build a portfolio with a professional beauty brand.



Schedule & Pay:

- Mon–Fri, 9:00 AM–6:00 PM Philippine time

- $600/month



HOW TO APPLY (Read Carefully) 

To ensure you are paying attention to detail, applications that do not follow these steps will be ignored.



Please reply to this job post with the subject line: "Creative Editor - [Your Name]" and include the following:

- Your video introduction.

- Your Portfolio: A link to your best work. Please highlight Short-Form Videos (TikTok/Reels).

- Your updated resume.



Hiring Process:

1. Submit Application

2. Shortlisted candidates do a simple creative test

3. Short video interview

4. Job Offer



We look forward to meeting you!

title: AI Video & Image Creative / Editor (Full-Time or Part-Time, Remote)
description:
About Us

We’re a new AI video and image creative agency + lab working at the crossroads of cinema, art, storytelling, and advertising.

We create cinematic and dynamic visuals for both commercial brands and artistic projects, combining creative vision with the latest AI video, image, and sound tools.

We’re a small, ambitious team building something exciting — equal parts production studio and experimental creative lab.



?



The Role



We’re looking for a Video & Image Creative / Editor who loves storytelling, rhythm, and visual design, and is excited to grow with the new wave of AI-powered creation.



You’ll help create and edit cinematic videos, image compositions, and AI-based visuals, working closely with our creative director and strategist.

You’ll use tools such as Runway, Pika, Veo3, Higgsfield, Midjourney, CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, and ChatGPT — and we’ll train you to master new ones as they emerge.



?



Requirements

 • Passion and experience in video editing or visual storytelling.

 • Basic familiarity with AI creative tools (Runway, Pika, Midjourney, etc.) or a strong willingness to learn.

 • Good eye for composition, light, rhythm, and cinematic feeling.

 • Comfortable following creative direction, SOPs, and feedback loops.

 • Communicative, reliable, and a genuine team player.

 • Basic audio-editing knowledge (or interest to learn).



?



Growth & Commitment

 • 30–40 hours per week (negotiable depending on skill level).

 • $600–$800 USD per month (negotiable).

 • Long-term role with real opportunities for growth — future positions include Senior Creative, R&D Specialist, or Art Director.

 • We support your training with AI video and image courses and access to premium creative tools.



?



To Apply



Please start your message with the word “Tommy” so we know you read the entire post carefully.



Then send:

 1. A few sentences about yourself and the stuff you like — your interests, inspirations, or creative influences.

 2. A short note on your experience and favorite tools.

 3. A link to your portfolio (Google Drive, YouTube, or website) showing your best AI edits, videos, or visuals.

 4. Your expected monthly rate (USD) and weekly availability.

CALLING HOURS

International shifts opening up across the world for various campaigns!

WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

The commissions are very generous with an UNLIMITED CAP ON YOUR POTENTIAL EARNINGS! All leads will be warm and provided to you through our CRM!



Apply now if you want to be part of something serious.



We look forward to helping you grow your career!



TO APPLY: Please COPY AND PASTE THE LINK BELOW INTO YOUR BROWSER! Fill out the form from there. 







Thank you!

title: AI Image Editing VA (Nano Banana / AI Generation)
description:

You will also be responsible for uploading the edited images directly to Shopify product pages, so Shopify experience is required.



This is a part-time position. The first task will likely last 1 to 4 days, but we regularly have AI image generation work and may continue working together if the collaboration goes well.



We also work with multiple store owners, so good workers may receive additional work opportunities.



Main Tasks



Edit 100 to 500 product images



• Replace the white models with black models using AI tools

• Keep the clothing product identical

• Maintain realistic lighting and proportions

• Upload the edited images to the correct Shopify product pages

• Ensure product images remain clean and professional



Requirements



You must have experience with:



• AI image generation tools (Nano Banana or similar)

• Realistic AI image editing

• Shopify product listing and media management (mandatory)



You must also:



• Be available at least 6 hours per day when needed

• Work efficiently and follow instructions carefully

• Communicate clearly and be reliable



Payment



$3 per hour



We know approximately how long these tasks take.



If someone lies about hours worked, we will stop working with them immediately.



We value honesty and efficiency.



Test Task (Required to Apply)



To apply, you must complete a small test task.



A Google Drive link with 5 product images is provided.





Your task:



• Replace the white model with a black model

• Keep the clothing product exactly the same

• Maintain realistic lighting and proportions



Once completed, reply to this job post with your edited images.



Applications without the completed test will not be reviewed.



When Applying

• The AI tools you use

• Your experience with Shopify product listings

?



? This is a long-term opportunity if you are good.

description:
